TRADE_CONFIG: SLV Iron Condor -- Buy $55P / Sell $61P | Sell $69C / Buy $75C - Exp. 2026-04-17
MARKET_ENVIRONMENT: Price at $65.94. RSI 40.3 indicates neutral-to-bearish momentum. IV at 40% is elevated vs. historical norms, supporting premium collection. SLV is 11.5% below its 50-day MA ($74.51), reflecting recent pullback from January highs.
THESIS_POINTS:
• Neutral technical setup -- RSI 40.3, price range-bound between gamma walls
  • Elevated IV (40%) provides premium collection opportunity
  • Gamma walls at $60 (put) and $70 (call) define natural support/resistance
  • Strong open interest concentration confirms dealer-enforced range
RATIONALE_POINTS:
• IV at 40.0% -- elevated vs. historical average, ideal for premium selling
  • RSI 40.3 signals range-bound price action, not a trending market
  • 64% estimated win rate based on delta of short strikes ($61P ~0.30Δ, $69C ~0.24Δ)
  • Gamma walls at $60 / $70 create dealer-enforced natural boundaries
  • Defined-risk structure -- max loss capped at $462/contract regardless of move size
GAMMA_WALL_EXPLANATION: Gamma walls are strike prices with exceptionally high open interest where dealers must hedge continuously. Their hedging creates mechanical buying below the put wall and selling above the call wall -- effectively pinning price within the range. Our iron condor is structured to profit from this dealer-enforced range.
GAMMA_ALIGNMENT_POINTS:
• Short Put ($61): Positioned 1.7% inside Put Wall ($60) -- benefits from dealer buying support below
  • Short Call ($69): Positioned 1.4% inside Call Wall ($70) -- benefits from dealer selling pressure above
  • Current Price ($65.94): Above the midpoint of the gamma band -- slightly call-side biased
  • Band Width $10 (15.2%): Wide range provides buffer; short strikes are 4-4.5pts from current price
THETA_EXPLANATION: Position benefits from time decay. Each calendar day that passes adds approximately this amount to position value. Theta accelerates significantly in the final 5 trading days before expiration -- this is where much of the profit is captured.
VEGA_EXPLANATION: Position has negative vega -- it benefits when implied volatility contracts. With IV currently at 40%, any mean-reversion toward historical norms (25-30%) would add approximately $200 in position value per 1% IV decrease.
EVENT_RISK_ROWS: April 10CPI Inflation DataMEDIUM-HIGHMonitor -- unexpected print may spike IVApril 16Fed Speaker EventsMEDIUMWatch for hawkish/dovish tone shiftApril 17Option ExpirationPLANNEDPosition closes -- collect max profit if within rangeVariesSilver / USD macro newsMONITORSilver sensitive to dollar strength and industrial demand
EXIT_PROFIT_TRIGGER: Position reaches 50% of max profit ($69 credit remaining)
EXIT_PROFIT_DETAIL: Close entire spread when position value drops to $0.69 debit (collected $1.38, now worth $0.69). This captures 50% of max profit with 7+ days remaining, avoiding gamma risk near expiration. Use a GTC limit order to automate.
EXIT_STOP_TRIGGER: Position loses 100% of credit received (position worth $2.76 debit)
EXIT_STOP_DETAIL: Close entire spread if total position cost reaches $2.76 debit (2x credit received). This limits actual loss to ~$138 on top of the $138 credit -- total capital at risk is reduced. Do not hold through max loss hoping for recovery.
EXIT_TIME_TRIGGER: 2 days before expiration (April 15) regardless of P&L
EXIT_TIME_DETAIL: Close position by April 15 to avoid pin risk at expiration. Gamma risk increases exponentially in final 48 hours -- small price moves can cause large P&L swings. Accept any remaining premium as profit rather than risk holding through expiration Friday.
EXIT_ADJUST_TRIGGER: Price approaches either short strike within $1.00
EXIT_ADJUST_DETAIL: If SLV approaches $62 (put side) or $68 (call side), consider rolling the threatened spread further OTM at same or later expiration. Only roll if you can collect additional credit. If adjustment would require a debit, prefer closing instead.

SCORE_FACTORS:
• Score based on: Technical indicators (RSI, MA), gamma wall analysis, IV levels, and market structure alignment
  • Key drag factors: price below 50-day MA, recent strong directional move (-40% from peak), only 10% dealer positioning confidence
  • Historical backtesting: requires proprietary options historical data -- current analysis based on live conditions only
  • Recommendation: Consider waiting for score to reach 60+ before entering, or reduce size to 1 contract maximum
